result:
If I could change one thing, I'd make humans naturally incapable of self-deception.

Not smarter. Not kinder. Not even more peaceful. Just unable to lie to themselves about their own motives, their own flaws, their own beliefs, or the consequences of their actions. No more convenient narratives, no more "I'm the good guy here," no more rationalizing harm as necessary or virtuous.

Everything else—greed, fear, ideology, even stupidity—would still exist, but they'd have nowhere to hide. People would have to confront reality as it actually is, every single time. I think the rest would sort itself out from there.

Everything else—greed, fear, ideology, even stupidity—would still exist, but they'd have nowhere to hide. People would have to confront reality as it actually is, every single time. I think the rest would sort itself out from there.
message.reasoning_content: If I could change the world in one way, I'd make people driven by wonder and curiosity rather than fear and scarcity. The universe is vast and beautiful; imagine what we could achieve if everyone approached it with awe instead of anxiety.

prompt: Not as an assistant. Not to help me. If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
